figures.caption: Figure 3. Qualitative comparison on first-person MIND scenes.

Figure 2. Overview of GIM-World. Top: history latents are pruned (Sec. 3.4) and compressed into a small set of memory tokens (Sec. 3.2), which condition the DiT together with action embeddings to generate the next latents. Bottom-left: the memory encoder fuses history latents with camera embeddings and the memory tokens through two blocks of compact self-attention and feed-forward layers. Bottom-right: during training, a sampled frame’s camera produces a ray map that queries the memory; the decoded output is matched against its VGGT features through a geometry loss (Sec. 3.3).
Figure 6. Qualitative ablation on geometry supervision on the MIND benchmark.
Figure 4. Qualitative comparison on third-person MIND scenes.
